subject: [RFC PATCH 12/45] mm: page_alloc: steer pageblock stealing to tainted superpageblocks

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's page allocation mechanism to prefer pages from tainted superpageblocks when stealing movable pageblocks for unmovable or reclaimable allocations. This is done by performing a bounded scan of the free list to find a tainted alternative if the original page belongs to a clean superpageblock. The goal is to concentrate contamination in already impure superpageblocks, preserving clean ones for future 1GB hugepage allocations.
